metadata.title: States PCS: Maharashtra GK Questions for MPSC Examinations

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Ambajogai is celebrated as the birthplace of Marathi literature. Mukundraj’s Vivekasindhu is the first literary work in Marathi. Ambajogai has hosted major literary gatherings such as the Ambajogai Sahitya Sammelan, attended by notable figures. The Vivekasindhu was penned in Ambajogai. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: Ambajogai is indeed celebrated for its significance in Marathi literary history. Mukundraj’s ‘Vivekasindhu’ is regarded as the first major literary work in Marathi, composed in Ambajogai. The city has hosted key literary gatherings, such as the Ambajogai Sahitya Sammelan, featuring figures like Sudarshan Rapatwar and Amar Habib. All four statements are factually correct as per historical and literary consensus.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: B G Kher was the first Chief Minister of Bombay. Yashwantrao Chavan was the first Chief Minister of Maharashtra state. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: Both statements are correct: B G Kher served as the first Chief Minister of Bombay Presidency (later Bombay State) from 1937 and post-independence from 1946 to 1952. Yashwantrao Chavan became the first Chief Minister of Maharashtra after its formation on 1 May 1960. Both leaders played pivotal roles in their respective regions’ history.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Jayaba Mukne was the first Koli ruler of Jawhar State. He established the Mukne Dynasty in 1306. Jawhar State ceased to exist after Indian independence in 1947. The Mukne Dynasty ruled for less than two centuries. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: Statements 1 and 2 are correct: Jayaba Mukne was the first Koli ruler of Jawhar, and he established the Mukne Dynasty in 1306. Statement 3 is also correct, as Jawhar State became part of India in 1947. Statement 4 is incorrect—the Mukne Dynasty ruled for over six centuries, not less than two.

questions.question: Consider the following statements: Sir M. Visvesvaraya is a recipient of the Bharat Ratna award. He played a significant role in the construction of the Krishna Raja Sagara Dam in Karnataka. He was instrumental in the establishment of several engineering colleges across India, promoting technical education. Which of the above statements is / are correct?
questions.explanation: Sir M. Visvesvaraya was awarded the Bharat Ratna in 1955 (statement 1). He played a pivotal role in constructing the Krishna Raja Sagara Dam (statement 2) and contributed significantly to establishing engineering colleges and promoting technical education in India (statement 3). Thus, all three statements are correct.

questions.question: Dnyaneshwari, authored by Sant Dnyaneshwar, is a celebrated commentary on which Hindu religious text?
questions.explanation: The Dnyaneshwari, written in the 13th century by Sant Dnyaneshwar, is a Marathi-language commentary on the Bhagavad Gita. It contains about 9,000 verses in the ovi meter and is a seminal work in Maharashtra’s spiritual and literary history.

questions.question: Which Peshwa conferred the prestigious title of ‘Deshmukh’ upon Kuroji Naik in the Maratha administration?
questions.explanation: Peshwa Madhavrao II granted the title of Deshmukh to Kuroji Naik for his loyalty and military service. Deshmukh was an administrative and feudal title in Maharashtra, pivotal for village governance and revenue collection during the Maratha regime.

questions.question: Which freedom fighters, born in Chinchwad, are known for assassinating British officer W.C. Rand?
questions.explanation: Chapekar Brothers—Damodar, Balkrishna, and Vasudev—were born in Chinchwad, Maharashtra. They assassinated British officer W.C. Rand in 1897 to protest British misrule during the plague in Pune. Their act marked a significant moment in India’s revolutionary freedom struggle against British colonial rule.

questions.question: Who was the first woman to embrace Buddhism during B. R. Ambedkar’s conversion movement?
questions.explanation: Savita Ambedkar, wife of B. R. Ambedkar, was the first woman to accept Buddhism at the 1956 Deekshabhoomi mass conversion. An MBBS graduate and physician, she played a crucial role in Ambedkar’s life and the Buddhist revival movement in India.

questions.question: Which Maratha ruler did the Bhoite family support in founding Hindavi Swarajya?
questions.explanation: The Bhoite family, prominent Maratha nobles, notably supported Chhatrapati Shivaji Maharaj in establishing Hindavi Swarajya, particularly during the Battle of Fort Subhanmangal (1648). They held social and military distinctions and remained loyal to the Maratha Empire until its fall in 1818.

questions.question: The 121st Pioneers were originally part of which army?
questions.explanation: The 121st Pioneers were originally part of the East India Company’s Bombay Army, formed in 1777 as the Marine Battalion. They participated in campaigns in Persia, Sindh, Punjab, Burma, and Abyssinia, and served in WWI. They were redesignated and later disbanded in 1932.
